rationale: The cited paper is used in the conceptual development of H3 as evidence that multichannel options can cannibalize sales from existing channels. This supports the citing paper's mechanism that transaction-oriented app features may act as channel substitutes and reduce the value impact of app launches. Although it is cited briefly and alongside other prior work, its role is to justify a key hypothesis rather than just provide a generic background list.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Ansari et al. (2008) as prior empirical support for channel cannibalization risk in multichannel settings. That evidence underpins the citing paper's prediction that transaction-focused branded apps can weaken the positive firm-value effect of app launch announcements.
